title: Global seaborne grain trade
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
While global seaborne grain trade prospects for the current 2021/22 season remain quite bullish, expectations for trade volumes have been lowered recently. The United States Department of Agriculture has released their latest forecasts for 2021/22 and is now forecasting global grain exports will total 507.5 million tons. This is 1.5 million tons less than was forecast a month ago but would mark a year-on-year increase of 25.3 million tons (5%).
